title: Wholesome Thai Butter Tofu Curry
description: A sweet and silky Thai curry with salted butter, braised tofu, and fresh veggies, perfect for a diabetic-friendly sick-day meal.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and melt the salted butter until foamy.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and ginger, cooking until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Mix in the Thai curry paste and cook for another minute, letting the flavors bloom.
Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable broth, stirring to combine the paste into a smooth sauce.
Add the cubed tofu and sliced bell pepper, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in fish sauce and erythritol, tasting and adjusting seasoning as needed.
Simmer uncovered for 8 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb flavors and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Finish by squeezing lime juice into the curry, stirring to meld the citrus notes.
Serve hot with steamed cauliflower rice or a side salad for a diabetic-friendly meal.
